name: Cloud data collection to access data via APIs using credentials
description: Collects user-related data from cloud services by issuing direct HTTP requests to public, internal, or undocumented API endpoints used by official client applications.
synonyms: Cloud Data Collection via Internal API
Cloud Data Collection via RESTful API
details: This technique collects user-related cloud data by capturing, analyzing, or emulating API request traffic generated by client applications. Requests may target public or undocumented endpoints identified via network observation or reverse engineering. Returned responses may include artifacts such as messages, file metadata, activity logs, and identity attributes. Analysts may use traffic capture tools or instrumentation to extract and store this data for forensic or intelligence purposes, both reactively and proactively.

references.relevance_summary_280: Open-source tool that implements this technique by inspecting and tracking RESTful API traffic from cloud client applications.
Proposes a hybrid cloud data acquisition approach that combines API-based collection with other methods.
Early example of API-based forensic acquisition of cloud user data, applied to the Amazon Alexa ecosystem.
